hero.headline:
Sense with Physical AI
Connect with Wireless AI
hero.description: In Physical AI, we apply VLA models to robotic manipulation and research model compression for edge deployment. In Wireless AI, our core topics are Wi-Fi CSI-based indoor localization and enhancing LoRa demodulation performance at the physical layer.
hero.panelText: We combine data-centric network design with smart IoT systems to deliver reliable, autonomous industrial connectivity.

cards.title: Wireless IoT Communication
cards.description: We study LPWA and resource allocation for diverse IoT service requirements.

cards.title: SON / KDN
cards.description: We develop AI-powered autonomous network control for planning, optimization, and self-healing.

cards.title: Smart Factory
cards.description: We build industrial IoT solutions for process monitoring, predictive maintenance, and object tracking.

research.heading: Exploring physical intelligence and wireless AI

areas.title: Physical AI
areas.description: We apply VLA (Vision-Language-Action) models to robotic manipulation and develop lightweight models optimized for edge deployment. Our work spans computer vision-based weld defect and anomaly detection, hierarchical reinforcement learning for autonomous control, and Wi-Fi Sensing for object shape recognition — covering the full spectrum of AI that perceives and acts in the physical world.

areas.title: Wireless AI
areas.description: We enhance LoRa preamble detection and symbol decoding under ultra-low SNR conditions using deep learning. Combined with Wi-Fi CSI-based indoor localization, LoRaWAN scheduling optimization, and IQ data compression for CRAN, we integrate AI/ML into the core of wireless communications to build more reliable IoT networks.

topics.title: VLA-Based Robot Arm Control
topics.description: Applying Vision-Language-Action models to robotic manipulation driven by natural language commands.
topics.detail:
#Background: Limits of Traditional Robotics & the Rise of Physical AI
Conventional robots handle only pre-programmed repetitive tasks, making them brittle in unexpected situations. Physical AI — where AI perceives and acts in the real world — is rapidly gaining attention. The key challenge is bridging natural-language commands and immediate physical actions.
#Our Approach: Lightweight VLA with Action Chunk Control
We adopt a VLA framework that links Vision, Language, and Action end-to-end. Using lightweight models such as smolVLA, we introduce Action Chunk Length Control — dynamically adjusting how many actions the robot plans at once based on task complexity.
#How It Works: Sim-to-Real Transfer with Data Augmentation
The robot takes visual input and text commands simultaneously to compute optimal joint movements. High-fidelity simulation environments and data augmentation train the model to generalize robustly to unseen real-world scenarios.
#Impact
Adaptive chunk control enables smoother, more efficient manipulation — paving the way for flexible service robots in smart factories and everyday life.

topics.title: JetBot-Based Autonomous Robot
topics.description: Researching autonomous indoor navigation with a small robot built on the NVIDIA Jetson platform.
topics.detail:
#Background: First Steps Toward Intelligent Autonomous Robots
This project is the Physical AI starting point — using the JetBot platform to build core technology that converts natural-language commands directly into physical driving behavior.
#Our Approach: JetBot-Tailored Fine-Tuning
We adopt a VLA architecture built on SmolVLM-500M-Instruct. Instead of retraining the entire model, we freeze the vision and language encoders and fine-tune only the JetBot Action Head that predicts wheel motor commands, maximizing efficiency.
#How It Works: Multimodal Perception to Wheel Control
The robot receives a real-time camera feed and a natural-language command together. The model fuses these inputs, and the Action Head predicts precise left/right motor speeds. Initial training leverages diverse driving data generated in the Isaac Sim 3D environment.
#Impact
This work proves that a lightweight AI model can translate human commands into real-time wheel control — a strong foundation for expanding toward fully autonomous indoor navigation without heavy compute.

topics.title: Wi-Fi CSI Indoor Localization
topics.description: Estimating precise indoor positions without GPS by analyzing channel state information (CSI).
topics.detail:
#Background: Environmental Fragility of Indoor Positioning
Existing Wi-Fi CSI-based localization is highly sensitive to geometric changes such as transmitter repositioning, severely limiting large-scale deployment in cost-constrained IoT environments with few antennas.
#Our Approach: Hybrid Model-Based Passive Channel Charting (PCC)
We propose a self-supervised PCC framework resilient to domain shifts. A hybrid CNN-Transformer architecture inside a Siamese Network captures both fine-grained local signal patterns and broad spatiotemporal correlations without location labels.
#How It Works: Joint Spatiotemporal Feature Learning
CNN extracts local patterns while the Transformer analyzes global dependencies. The model maps high-dimensional CSI data to a low-dimensional spatial coordinate system, requiring no labeled position data during training.
#Impact
Validated in real indoor environments, the system maintains low localization error even across unseen transmitter layouts — proving reliable positioning is achievable with commodity low-antenna IoT devices.

topics.title: Vision-Based Anomaly Detection
topics.description: Automated weld defect and manufacturing anomaly detection using unsupervised learning and computer vision.
topics.detail:
#Background: Limits of Automated Weld Inspection
Radiographic testing (RT) in shipbuilding still relies on slow manual expert evaluation. Automation requires extracting weld regions that vary by pipe size, while defect samples are extremely scarce — often under 1% of all data.
#Our Approach: Unified Framework on Minimal Data
We integrate few-shot segmentation for weld region extraction and unsupervised anomaly detection trained on normal data only — combining both into a single end-to-end framework.
#How It Works: Defect Synthesis & Skip-Connection Restoration
A deep learning model synthesizes artificial defects on normal images and learns to restore them. Skip-connection architecture ensures fine normal textures are precisely reconstructed while real defects stand out clearly, pinpointing defect locations.
#Impact
The system delivers visual defect maps without large defect datasets or pixel annotations. At 88.2 FPS, it is ready for direct deployment in real industrial settings.

topics.title: LoRa Physical Layer AI
topics.description: Improving preamble detection and symbol decoding performance under ultra-low SNR with deep learning.
topics.detail:
#Background: Structural Limits of LoRa Under Extreme Noise
LoRa excels at long-range transmission, but in ultra-low SNR environments it fails to detect the signal preamble — the starting point of each frame — causing reception failures.
#Our Approach: Self-Attention-Based Signal Analysis
We apply a Transformer-based self-attention architecture that analyzes global linear patterns across fragmented chirp signals, effectively separating meaningful signal from noise.
#How It Works: Restore-then-Detect Pipeline
Deep learning suppresses noise and reconstructs a clean symbol sequence close to the original. The refined sequence is then used to determine the presence and exact position of the preamble.
#Impact
Validated at extreme noise levels (−21.7 to −24.3 dB), the system achieves over 90% stable detection accuracy — improving receiver reliability in critical scenarios such as mountain rescue and maritime operations.

topics.title: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning
topics.description: Solving complex long-horizon decision problems through automatic subgoal discovery and value-gradient exploration.
topics.detail:
#Background: Logistics Bottlenecks in Shipyard Block Storage
In shipyard block assembly, congestion arises from storing and retrieving large hull blocks in temporary yards. Conventional heuristics and standard RL fail to account for how today's placement decisions affect future retrieval operations.
#Our Approach: Two-Head Hierarchical RL (HRL)
We decompose the Planar Storage Location Assignment Problem (PSLAP) into two levels: an Option Controller that plans macro-level storage strategies with a long-term view, and a Primitive Controller that executes fine-grained movement actions.
#How It Works: Delay-Aware Reward for Long-Horizon Planning
Instead of rewarding only immediate placement, the system traces outcomes all the way to final retrieval and assigns rewards accordingly. A specialized replay memory lets the agent optimize both immediate execution and long-term operational efficiency.
#Impact
The system maintains strong stability in densely packed yards and large-scale scenarios — establishing a solid foundation for real-world shipyard decision-support systems.

items.title: Attending Conferences Worldwide
items.description: The lab supports attendance at domestic conferences and, based on your research progress, international ones too. On the ground at a conference, you engage directly with the latest work from researchers worldwide and sharpen your perspective through talks and live discussion. Presenting your own work, or simply talking with researchers on similar topics, lets you stress-test your ideas and explore unexpected directions — a kind of growth that papers alone cannot provide.

items.title: Overseas Research Lab Visit
items.description: Open to PhD researchers, this program leverages the advisor's international network to arrange visits of up to one month at partner institutions in Japan, Indonesia, and beyond. Working alongside local research teams on collaborative projects, you develop a first-hand sense of how research is done in a global environment.

items.title: Government & Industry R&D
items.description: Participate in funded projects from MSIT and MND, as well as industry partnerships — applying research to solve real-world engineering problems.

items.title: Hands-On Technical Skills
items.description: PyTorch deep learning, ROS robot control, GNU Radio & SDR signal processing, LoRa hardware — skills that hold up in both academic research and industry.

items.title: The Research-to-Paper Process
items.description: Participating in active lab projects takes you through the full research cycle — problem framing, experiments, analysis, and writing. Results that meet the standard open doors to conference presentations or SCI journal submissions, with first authorship as a real possibility.
